claim: QuickAdd by chhoumann is at 2.2k GitHub stars with v2.12.0 released March 5 2026, bundling Captures, Templates, Macros, and Multi choices.
quote: QuickAdd is an Obsidian plugin combining four tools called choices: Captures, Templates, Macros, and Multi choices ... 2.2k stars ... Latest Release: Version 2.12.0 (March 5, 2026)

claim: QuickAdd's Capture choice appends content to predefined files such as appending a link to today's daily note under a specific section.
quote: A Capture allows you to quickly add content to predefined files. For example, you could set up a quick action to add a link to the open file to your daily note under a specific section.

claim: Templater by SilentVoid13 is at 4.9k GitHub stars and actively maintained with v2.20.0 released April 29 2026, supporting JS execution and dynamic variables in templates.
quote: GitHub Stars: 4.9k ... latest release: 2.20.0 on Apr 29, 2026 ... defines a templating language that lets you insert variables and functions results into your notes

claim: Templater can bind a template to a hotkey via the Template Hotkeys setting, hidden until 'Trigger Template on new file creation' is enabled.
quote: Template Hotkeys allows you to bind a template to a hotkey. This setting is hidden by default. To view it first enable the Trigger Template on new file creation setting.

claim: Periodic Notes (liamcain) sits at 1.3k stars but its last public release is the 1.0.0-beta.3 from April 2022 and the community widely treats it as effectively unmaintained.
quote: 1.3k stars and 107 forks ... The latest release (1.0.0-beta.3) is dated April 14, 2022

claim: The Journals plugin (srg-kostyrko) is the actively-maintained 2025/2026 replacement for Periodic Notes, supporting daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly plus interval-based journals and offering bulk import from Periodic Notes.
quote: deactivated the calendar plugin, Periodic Notes & Daily Notes and everything seems to work with the Journals plugin only

claim: Sébastien Dubois's 2026 must-have list pairs QuickAdd for capture with Templater for note typing, plus Rollover Daily Todos and Auto Note Mover for inbox processing.
quote: design clearly defined note types, then templates for each of those making it much easier to be systematic in the way you take notes

claim: The dominant power-user pattern is QuickAdd plus the Global Hotkeys plugin, optionally augmented with Alfred/ULauncher, with Ctrl+Alt+A as a common trigger.
quote: I use both: I use the App Launcher when I am doing focused work, and the global hotkey whenever I don't mind switching contexts to provide a little more organization.

claim: On Windows, an AutoHotkey script bound to Ctrl+Alt+V activates Obsidian if running (or launches it) then sends Ctrl+Alt+C to open QuickAdd's capture window for system-wide capture.
quote: IfWinExist, ahk_exe Obsidian.exe...WinActivate, Obsidian.exe ... checks if the program is already running

claim: A widely-shared inbox-processing pattern combines Advanced URI, Templater, and Hotkeys for Templates with Opt+A/D/M bindings for archive, trash, and move on each captured note.
quote: I was putting some of my fleeting ideas in a system that would never be resurfaced

claim: Thino (1.3k stars, v3.0.10 April 2026) is a Twitter-style quick-capture surface that posts time-stamped entries into the daily note, but went closed-source from v2.0.0 with paid pro features.
quote: From version 2.0.0 onward, Thino is closed-source ... the input transforms into formatted entries like '- 22:15 {your input content}' within daily notes

claim: The dominant Obsidian inbox idiom is to treat today's daily note as the universal capture target and process it during review rather than at capture time.
quote: Daily notes act as a central inbox and daily dashboard ... Today's note becomes your inbox, and processing happens during review time rather than during capture.
